title: ‘House of Gucci’ and Top 5 Ridley Scott Movies
format: Sean, Amanda, and Chris Ryan on Ridley Scott's House of Gucci, then their three-way run at his best films; plus Sean's interview with DMX: Don't Try to Understand director Chris Frierson.

films.title: House of Gucci
films.note: Adapted from Sara Gay Forden's 2001 book. Lady Gaga as Patrizia, Adam Driver as Maurizio, with Al Pacino, Jared Leto, Jeremy Irons, and Salma Hayek. The group reads it as a high-class, in-on-the-joke soap opera: Gaga setting the over-the-top tone, Driver playing the restrained center, and Leto's near-unrecognizable Paolo as the sad, broken heart of the thing. Ridley almost made it in 2006 with DiCaprio and Angelina Jolie, and again in 2016 with Wong Kar-wai directing Margot Robbie.

segments.heading: The Ridley Scott experience
segments.note: the man, the motor, the filmography

films.title: The Last Duel
films.note: His other 2021 film: American cast doing a mid-transatlantic quasi-English accent, versus Gucci's American cast doing Italian.

films.title: All the Money in the World
films.note: Another portrait of rich, empty people in the European countryside; Kevin Spacey was originally Getty before the reshoots with Christopher Plummer.

films.title: A Good Year
films.note: Ridley shooting a movie down the road from the Provence house he owns. Amanda tried hard to make it a top-five pick and couldn't: 'it's not good.'

films.title: Exodus: Gods and Kings
films.note: One of his least successful, but Sean owns the Blu-ray as part of a personal project to map a century of American studio movies, and to give it another chance someday.

films.title: Robin Hood
films.note: The 2010 outing Sean and Chris saw together: the seed of an early, since-lost Ridley Scott podcast they recorded in a Park Slope basement.

films.title: 1492: Conquest of Paradise
films.note: One of his famous bombs, from the 1992-96 stretch when he didn't release a film.

segments.heading: Top 5 Ridley Scott Movies
segments.note: each host's five, worst to first (No. 1 and No. 2 agreed by all three)

picks.title: Alien
picks.note: No. 1, unanimous. Only his second film; a spare, terrifying workplace drama that everyone's tried and failed to reproduce for 40 years.

picks.title: Blade Runner
picks.note: No. 2, unanimous. Forty years old and still looks like it was made yesterday: a whole visual language invented in a handful of shots.

picks.title: Gladiator
picks.note: No. 3. A monoculture moment he could get behind: Russell Crowe is Maximus and always will be.

picks.title: Matchstick Men
picks.note: No. 4. A hardcore black comedy con movie with Nicolas Cage and Sam Rockwell, editing that mirrors Cage's compulsions; Ted Griffin script at his Ocean's Eleven peak.

picks.title: The Counselor
picks.note: No. 5. A preposterous Cormac McCarthy script and a cast chewing scenery. Ridley happy to serve someone else's material and still shoot it beautifully.

picks.title: Thelma & Louise
picks.note: No. 3. Ridley finding the comedy and warmth in a friendship movie with dark themes, plus one of the most famous shots in movies.

picks.title: The Martian
picks.note: No. 4. 'Apollo 13 on Mars': a warm, competent problem-solving movie, an unusually crowd-pleasing turn for a director whose early work is about how humans suck in space.

picks.title: House of Gucci
picks.note: No. 5. Exactly what she wants Ridley spending his money and movie stars on: beautiful people in Italy wearing Gucci.

picks.title: Kingdom of Heaven
picks.note: No. 3, the director's cut specifically: 'full Kubrick,' great battle sequences, Edward Norton as a masked leper king. A regular rewatch for Chris and his mom.

picks.title: Black Hawk Down
picks.note: No. 4. His modern war film: Tony Scott-style handheld chaos where you always know where you are, and a cast packed with future stars (there's Tom Hardy, three lines).

picks.title: Prometheus
picks.note: No. 5, a twofer with Alien: Covenant, late-period Alien entries Chris thinks are drastically underrated for being box-office 'failures.'

picks.title: Alien: Covenant
picks.note: No. 5, paired with Prometheus. Chris revisited it for the pod and was blown away: the first hour scarier than almost any horror movie.

segments.heading: Chris Frierson on ‘DMX: Don't Try to Understand’

films.title: DMX: Don't Try to Understand
films.note: The Music Box installment following Earl 'DMX' Simmons across a year after his 2019 release from prison. Frierson on earning DMX's trust by never asking about rap, letting his life unfold without talking-head interviews, and finishing the film before his death.

All the Money in the World.title: All the Money in the World
